questions.question: Explain the concept of an operating system as a resource manager. (3 marks)
keyPoints: Manages hardware resources like CPU, memory, I/O devices
Allocates resources fairly and efficiently among competing processes
Prevents conflicts and ensures system stability through protection

questions.question: Compare and contrast the responsibilities of the Operating System in Process Management and Memory Management. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Process Mgmt: Focuses on the CPU (scheduling, creation, termination, IPC).
Memory Mgmt: Focuses on RAM (allocation, deallocation, protection).
Both are essential for multitasking and process isolation.
They are interrelated: a process must be loaded into memory to execute.
Process Mgmt deals with 'when' a process runs, Memory Mgmt deals with 'where' its instructions/data are stored.
